chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. Cr. Bail Appl No.1828 of 2019 Date Order with signature of Judge Qamar Bhatti........................................................................Applicant Versus The State........................................................................Respondent For hearing of bail application 13.01.2020 Mr. Nadeem Ahmed Azar, Advocate for Applicant Mr. Ali Haider Salim, DPG O R D E R Applicant is seeking post arrest bail in a case bearing Crime No.308/2019, U/s 23(I)-A SAA, registered at Police Station Shah Faisal Colony, Karachi by means of this application. 2. Applicant along with three co-accused was allegedly arrested on 02.09.2019 at about 0200 hours from Malir River, Shah Faisal Colony Karachi. From applicant 15 cartridges of 12 bore were recovered, whereas from remaining accused, one rifle grenade each was secured. Accordingly, all the accused were booked in separate cases. 3. Learned defence counsel has argued that applicant is innocent and has been falsely implicated in this case; that prior to filing of the present case, the relative of the applicant had moved application to various authorities for his illegal detention by Ranger officials and only in order to cover up that the applicant has been implicated in this case. 4. On the other hand, learned DPG has opposed grant of bail to the applicant but has admitted that challan has been submitted and the applicant is no more required for further investigation. 5. We have considered submissions of the parties and perused the material available on record. There is recovery of 15 cartridges of 12 bore from the applicant without any weapon.

We have considered submissions of the parties and perused the material available on record. There is recovery of 15 cartridges of 12 bore from the applicant without any weapon. The investigation has already been completed and the challan has been submitted. The applicant is no more required for further investigation. There is no material either to show any previous criminal record of the applicant. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety 2 in the sum of Rs.1,00,000/ (Rupees one lac) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 6. The bail application is disposed of in the above terms; the findings made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. Judge Judge Rafiq P.A.
